# Manage Assets

Create, update, organize, move, and explicitly delete reusable assets.

## Procedure

scope

- Classify the operation as list, search, inspect, create, update, move, organize, or delete.
- Resolve the asset kind and asset id when applicable.
- Treat list, search, and inspect as read-only operations.

inspect

- Read existing asset directories under `.wonder/reuse/`.
- Validate each managed asset has `asset.json` and `body.md`.
- Check references before moving or deleting assets.
- Report unused or questionable assets only as deletion candidates.

change

- Create, update, move, organize, or delete assets only for changing operations.
- Write `asset.json` with id, kind, title, description, variables, and optional metadata.
- Use `{}` for `variables` when an asset has no variables.
- Deletion is allowed only when explicitly requested by the user.
- Referenced asset deletion requires explicit confirmation.
- Never delete deletion candidates automatically.

index

- Regenerate `.wonder/reuse/index.json` from asset directories after changing operations.
- If the index is missing or corrupt, regenerate it from asset directories.
- Preserve asset files if index regeneration fails.

report

- For changing operations, write `.wonder/runs/<run-id>/asset-changes.json`.
- Summarize assets created, updated, moved, explicitly deleted, and deletion candidates.
- Report index refresh status.

Wonder Reuse does not maintain a latest report file.
Do not render assets into final user output.
Do not promote ordinary project output into reusable assets.
Do not delete assets without explicit user request.
Do not modify other plugin config files.
